name: Cinderbun
concept: A loaf-shaped hearth creature made of dense wool felt with a live coal quietly burning inside its belly — it keeps its own little fire warm by resting both stubby paws on its slowly-toasting tummy.
imagePrompt:
A single chunky plush creature called Cinderbun, one connected body, standing upright and facing the viewer straight on, full body visible including feet.

SILHOUETTE AND PROPORTIONS: One solid rounded loaf-shape — a fat bread-roll or smooth river pebble, slightly wider than it is tall, with a gently domed top. Head and body are one undivided mass (no neck). Two thick rounded nubs rise from the top corners of the dome like the folded corners of a blanket. Two short sausage-shaped arms sewn onto the sides, both stubby paws resting flat on its belly. Two stumpy legs, very short, ending in wide flat oval felt feet that plant it firmly on the ground. The whole outline is a clean, soft, unbroken curve: no spikes, no strands, no floating parts, no sparks, no smoke, no particles, nothing detached from the body. Every edge is a crisp cut felt edge, not fuzzy.

MATERIAL: Dense, matte, tightly compacted wool felt — the weave and needle-felted fibre grain clearly legible, like a well-made handmade toy. Thick cream-coloured blanket-stitch seams run over the top of the head and down the outer sides, big visible chunky stitches. Slight pleasant sag and weight, softly stuffed, gentle dents where the paws press into the belly. One small darker charcoal felt patch is stitched onto the left shoulder like a loving repair.

PALETTE: One bold hero colour — deep glowing ember orange (warm, saturated, like lit coal). The orange is strongest at the belly and face and deepens through burnt sienna into a soft charcoal-plum at the back, feet and lower edges, as if the creature is a coal slowly cooling at the edges. A pale toasty oatmeal-cream felt panel covers the belly. Accents in soft ash grey and dark cocoa brown thread. Warm, cosy, hearth-lit.

THE ONE IDEA — the belly hearth: A large round quilted panel of cream felt is stitched into the chest like a little oven door. Three thick embroidered bars cross it like a hearth grate, and behind them the felt glows a hot amber-gold, as though a coal burns inside the stuffing. The cream felt immediately around the panel is toasted to a caramel-brown scorch ring, gently darkening outward — the warmth has been slowly baking the fabric from the inside for years. The creature's two paws rest against this warm belly, holding its own heat like someone cupping a mug of tea.

FACE: Big, round, glossy black button eyes — real sewn-on shank buttons, wide-set and low on the face, each with a single clean white highlight. Between them, a small soft muzzle bump. The mouth is a simple curved line of dark cocoa running-stitch, a calm contented closed smile. Two warm rosy-pink felt cheek patches, plain ovals, stitched on. The expression is honest, sleepy, patient and kind — a face that wants to be looked after.

Everything bold and chunky: no detail finer than a thick stitch, no text, no symbols, no thin hairs or wisps. Toy-photography softness, clear readable shapes, lovable and huggable.
